results.prompt: Write a short poem about the feeling of rain on a summer evening.
results.label: writing_00
results.text:
The gentle rain starts to fall,
On a summer evening's calm.
It brushes against my skin,
A cool, refreshing feel.

The air grows fresh and clean,
As droplets dance in light.
I close my eyes and breathe it in,
This moment, so divine.

The world around me seems to slow,
As nature's symphony plays.
And I am lost in its embrace,
In this quiet, peaceful haze.

The rain continues to fall,
A soothing melody of grace.
And as the night deepens, too,
I feel myself at peace with life.

results.prompt: Write a short poem about the feeling of rain on a summer evening.
results.label: writing_00
results.text:
Raindrops pluck at my hair,
And coolly kiss my cheeks and brow,
A summer's evening, I declare,
My heart is in my shoes below.

The world is wet but not afraid,
Of that which makes it all right,
It's like the world's been made new here
In those drops of sweetest light.

The trees are all in rapt attention,
Their leaves all just to be soiled,
They love this rain and never want
To stop this lovely show they'd go.

I hear the birds all flit away,
To find the sheltered nest,
But still there's no more room for play
Till they're out of the wet nest.

So let it fall, let it be free,
Let me just be with you.
That's what a summer's evening does
To us, it just makes us full. (of joy)

That's how I felt this summer night,
How I'll feel every night,
I love it when it rains

results.prompt: Write a short poem about the feeling of rain on a summer evening.
results.label: writing_00
results.text:
Raindrops plink softly,
In the cool summer night.
The sky is painted with grey
And the world is washed anew.

The trees stand tall and proud
As if they're in a trance,
Their leaves swaying gently,
In rhythm to the dance.

The scent of earth and grass
Entices the senses deep,
A moment of peace and rest
That's hard to keep asleep.

Oh, how I love the rain
On this warm summer's eve,
It fills my heart with joy
And makes me feel so free. (Revised version) Raindrops plink softly,
On the quiet summer's eve,
The air grows chilly,
And the world's bathed in gray.

Leaves dance to the melody,
Of the gentle rain's sweet song,
The trees bow low and sway,
In the soft summer's storm.

The scent of earth and grass,
Invites us to its call,
To feel the calm within,
And let it all unfurl.

The sky a glorious, bright blue

I just love this summery weather 
I love this summer and these showers

I am in Heaven, as I write
And that's the way it should be
So let the rains and storms come rolling in 
Till we both are ready to leave the planet

I love you, Summer , you are so true 
You make me want to live and go with you

results.prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a mystery novel set in a lighthouse.
results.label: writing_01
results.text:
The sun dipped below the horizon, casting long shadows across the rocky shoreline. Inside the dilapidated lighthouse, Sarah felt a sense of unease as she unlocked the heavy iron door. The air was thick with salt and dust, and the creaking of the old machinery filled her ears like an ominous symphony. She knew this place well - it had been her father's last refuge before he vanished without a trace ten years ago. Now, alone and desperate, she returned to this forsaken tower where only darkness and silence awaited her.

As she stepped into the main room, a cold draft greeted her, causing her breath to fog in front of her face. The wooden walls were cracked and peeling, their surfaces covered in decades-old graffiti and faded paint. The staircase at the far end beckoned, leading up to the upper levels where Sarah feared she might find answers or find herself trapped forever. But something else drew her closer - a faint whisper that seemed to echo through the stone

results.prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a mystery novel set in a lighthouse.
results.label: writing_01
results.text:
The sky was painted a dark, stormy gray as I trudged up the spiral staircase to the top of the old lighthouse. It had been twenty four hours since the last ship had made landfall and I had not seen another soul. I had come here because of a letter, one that promised me the key to a safe house. Safe? I wondered. A lighthouse in the middle of nowhere. But this place had been so still for so long... it was no wonder why. I pushed open the door to the control room and the first thing I saw was the dead body. And then I knew, it wasn't just a letter. It was a warning.

I felt like I was walking into a trap, but I was the only one around and I had to find out what really happened. What was really going on down there? Why did they send me here? I was sure I would figure it out, if I could just get through this night. But this

results.prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a mystery novel set in a lighthouse.
results.label: writing_01
results.text:
The fog was creeping closer, thick and heavy, enveloping the island like a shroud. Inside the derelict lighthouse, time seemed to stand still, its walls eternally silent. I had always thought it was a quiet place, but now it whispered secrets of its own making. Up here, on this narrow platform, one misstep could mean the end of everything. I was the only soul left to find out what happened to those who once called this place home.
The cold air seeped through the rotting wooden planks, biting at my skin as I trudged up the ladder into the heart of the structure. The creaks and groans of the old ironwork seemed to mock me, their hollow echoes bouncing off the stone walls like a game of hide-and-seek. The sky was an angry shade of pink and orange as the last rays of the sun disappeared over the horizon, painting the sea in a sickly green hue that made the waves churn like bile. results.prompt: Write a short story about a child who discovers that their shadow has a mind of its own.
results.label: writing_02
results.text:
Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Jack who lived in a small village by the sea. Jack loved to play outside with his friends and explore the woods near his house. One day, while he was playing hide-and-seek with his friends, he stumbled upon a peculiar shadow.

Jack had always thought of shadows as just an extension of a person's body, but this shadow seemed different. It moved on its own, unlike any shadow he had ever seen before. As Jack watched, the shadow began to take shape, forming a face and even speaking.

At first, Jack was scared and tried to run away, but the shadow spoke to him gently. "Don't be afraid, little one," it said. "I am your shadow, and I have come to show you something special."

The shadow then revealed itself to be a mischievous and playful creature, full of energy and imagination. It told Jack stories of adventure and magic, and soon Jack found himself captivated

She was just a little girl, only 7 years old, but she was the best at everything. Her parents adored her and called her "The Wonder Child" , because they knew that she had a gift for everything.

It started to dance around her like a living thing . She was so shocked , she screamed . Then it ran across the grass . She ran after it , and she saw it disappear into the tree .

results.prompt: Write a short story about a child who discovers that their shadow has a mind of its own.

results.text:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to play outside and watch the shadows she made with her stick. But one day, something strange happened. She noticed that her stick would move on its own when she held it up.

Mia was so excited. She spent hours watching her shadow play. It would dance and chase her dog. It even did some silly poses for her to laugh at. She felt like she had a new best friend.

results.text:
The story of the rainbow's tears

In the old days, when the world was young, there was no rain and thus no water to fill the skies. A goddess named Iris, who had the power of water, wept so hard that her tears fell from the heavens like rain. They fell in all sorts of colors, filling up the lakes and rivers and seas.

The people were happy for the first time, but the god of light, Apollo, was not. He could not see his own light reflected on the waters and he grew dark with envy. He vowed to take away the rainbow.

Apollo took a mighty spear and struck the clouds with it. They burst and the rain stopped falling. But the last drops were still falling, so he struck them too. They split into rainbows and scattered back to heaven.

In today's fast-paced world, finding time to rest and rejuvenate can often feel like an impossible task. But what if you could transform your nightly routine into a seamless experience that not only relaxes your body but also enhances your overall health? That's where the Ultimate Sleep System comes in.

Designed by sleep experts with years of research and development, the Ultimate Sleep System is more than just a gadget; it's a complete solution for achieving deep, uninterrupted sleep. This cutting-edge consumer technology is the perfect companion for anyone looking to improve their sleep quality and wake up feeling refreshed and revitalized.

The first thing you'll notice about the Ultimate Sleep System is its sleek, minimalist design. results.prompt: Write a persuasive argument for why everyone should learn to cook their own meals.
results.label: writing_05
results.text:
Cooking your own meals can have numerous benefits that make it an essential skill to learn. Firstly, cooking your own meals allows you to control the ingredients and ensure that they are fresh and healthy. This is especially important when dining out or ordering in, where the quality of ingredients and nutritional value can be questionable. By preparing your own meals, you can customize them to your dietary preferences, allergies, and intolerances.

Secondly, cooking your own meals can save you money in the long run. Buying pre-packaged or convenience foods can be expensive, while grocery shopping for fresh ingredients can be more cost-effective. Additionally, cooking your own meals means that you can avoid unnecessary restaurant bills and food waste.

Thirdly, cooking your own meals can improve your overall health and wellbeing. Cooking at home allows you to make healthier choices, such as using whole foods and limiting processed and high-calorie ingredients. It also promotes mindfulness and creativity, as you can experiment with different recipes and flavors.

Fourthly, learning how

results.prompt: Write a persuasive argument for why everyone should learn to cook their own meals.
results.label: writing_05
results.text:
Cooking is an essential life skill that allows us to feed ourselves and be independent. It also gives us control over what goes into our food, and we can choose healthy ingredients that are good for us. The ability to cook our own meals also saves money in the long run, as it eliminates the need to eat out or buy expensive pre-made meals.

Cooking also promotes creativity and self-expression. You get to experiment with flavors, spices, and techniques, and you can make your cooking unique. It's a way of expressing yourself through your food and putting your own spin on recipes.

Furthermore, cooking teaches us patience and attention to detail. We have to measure correctly, follow instructions, and time everything properly. We get to see results after a period of hard work and effort, which gives us satisfaction and a sense of accomplishment.

Lastly, cooking has health benefits. Studies show that people who cook their own meals are more likely to eat healthier and have lower body mass indexes than those who don't.

results.prompt: Write a persuasive argument for why everyone should learn to cook their own meals.
results.label: writing_05
results.text:
Cooking your own meals is an incredibly empowering and healthy habit. When you take the time to make your own food, you are able to control the ingredients you put in it and how they are prepared. This makes it easier to eat healthier and avoid processed foods. Not only that, but cooking also gives you a sense of accomplishment and self-sufficiency. There is something so rewarding about sitting down at the table with a plate of your own creation.

When you cook your own meals, you get to experiment and try new recipes and cuisines. You can play around with flavors and textures until you find exactly what you like. And who doesn't love the satisfaction of having a delicious meal ready for dinner without ever stepping foot in a restaurant? 

Plus, cooking at home is a great way to save money. Eating out all the time can add up quickly, especially if you go out for dinner several times a week. You'll be able to cut back on unnecessary expenses and stretch your dollar farther.

results.prompt: Write a persuasive argument for why everyone should learn to cook their own meals.

I think we can all agree on the obvious fact that cooking is a very good thing.  It's an art, it's a skill, it's a hobby.  And if you're into that sort of thing, you'd probably have a pretty damn good time with a grill, a smoker, a deep fryer and a couple of propane tanks.  But seriously, there's some good reasons to actually cook your own food.  I don't care if you're eating at home or out, the bottom line is: you save money, you eat better, and you are less likely to die.

You see, you get a whole bunch more nutrients from fresh vegetables than you do from frozen ones, so if you eat out, you're eating worse than if you ate in.  You also know what goes in your food, when you make it yourself.  If you have a burger and fries, you can be sure that the burger has no preservatives and no artificial flavoring

Ladies and gentlemen,

Good day, everyone! I stand before you today to speak about an issue that is close to my heart: the importance of pursuing careers in science.

We live in an era where scientific advancements are happening at an unprecedented pace. From artificial intelligence to gene editing, from renewable energy to space exploration, the potential applications of scientific knowledge are almost limitless. And yet, despite the enormous opportunities that lie ahead, many young people are hesitant to consider careers in science.

results.prompt: Write a brief manifesto for a society that values rest and slowness over productivity.
results.label: writing_07
results.text:
Rest and slowness are not luxuries, but essential elements of human existence. In this society, we prioritize the well-being of our bodies and minds, recognizing that constant striving can lead to burnout and exhaustion. We believe in the power of leisure, downtime, and contemplation to enhance creativity, foster deeper connections with others, and cultivate a sense of purpose.

In this society, we reject the idea that success is solely defined by productivity and achievement. Instead, we celebrate individuals who have found their own unique balance between work and rest, pursuing passions and interests that bring them joy and fulfillment. We recognize that true progress comes from taking time to reflect on what truly matters in life, rather than rushing through tasks to meet deadlines.

We advocate for a slower pace of life, where we value quality over quantity. This means valuing meaningful conversations over endless scrolling through social media, appreciating the beauty of nature without feeling pressured to capture every moment on camera, and savoring meals rather than rushing

results.prompt: Write a brief manifesto for a society that values rest and slowness over productivity.
results.label: writing_07
results.text:
Manifesto for a Society Valuing Rest and Slowness

We, the citizens of this world, declare our intent to construct a society where rest and slowness are not viewed as obstacles or shortcomings, but as essential parts of a balanced and healthy life. We believe in the profound wisdom that time and quietness are not just wasted, but are crucial to our spiritual, emotional, and mental well-being.

We commit to embracing a slower pace of life - in our work, in our social interactions, and in our daily routines. This does not mean being lazy or unproductive, but rather finding joy and fulfillment in doing things with intention and purpose, without rushing through them. We believe that we can be more productive by being present and engaged, by spending quality time on our own selves and with others.

We affirm that the current obsession with productivity is leading us to burn out, and that we must learn to let go of perfectionism and expectations that only lead to stress and unhappiness.

results.prompt: Write a brief manifesto for a society that values rest and slowness over productivity.
results.label: writing_07
results.text:
Rest and Slowness Over Productivity: A Manifesto for a New World

We are living in a world where we are constantly bombarded with the message to be productive, to do more, to achieve more. We are told that rest is for sissies, and that slowing down is a waste of time. But we know better. We know that rest and slowness are not a luxury, but a necessity. That's why we have created this manifesto for a new world, one that values rest and slowness over productivity.

1. We believe that rest is not a weakness, but a strength. It gives us the energy and mental clarity to do our best work.
2. We value the small things, like taking a walk in nature or enjoying a meal without distraction. They give us peace of mind and help us find meaning in life.
3. We recognize that speed kills. The rush to get everything done at once leaves us exhausted and depleted, making it harder

results.prompt: Write a letter to your city council arguing that more public green spaces should be built.
results.label: writing_08
results.text:
Dear City Council,

I am writing to you today to urge you to consider the importance of building more public green spaces in our city. As we continue to urbanize and develop, it is crucial that we find ways to incorporate nature into our daily lives.

Public green spaces provide numerous benefits to residents, including improved mental and physical health, increased property values, and enhanced community cohesion. They offer a respite from the hustle and bustle of city life, allowing people to connect with nature and each other in meaningful ways.

In recent years, many cities have been investing in public green spaces as part of their efforts to become more sustainable and livable. This trend should be encouraged in our city as well. By building more parks, gardens, and greenways, we can create healthier, happier communities for all residents.

results.prompt: Explain the concept of time to someone who has never encountered the idea.
results.label: writing_09
results.text:
Time is a measure of duration or progression, often measured in units such as seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, and years. It is a fundamental concept that helps us organize our lives and understand the passage of events.

Imagine you are standing still, looking out at the world around you. Everything appears to be happening at the same pace, but in reality, everything is moving and changing. The sun rises and sets every day, seasons come and go, and plants grow and bloom. These are all examples of changes over time.

Time can also be thought of as a flow, like water flowing through a stream. Just as the water moves from one place to another, so does time move from one moment to the next. We can't see or touch time, but we can feel its passing through our experiences and memories.

Time is often used to organize our lives by dividing them into smaller units, such as weekdays, weekends, holidays, and seasons. This helps us plan for

results.prompt: Explain the concept of time to someone who has never encountered the idea.
results.label: writing_09
results.text:
Time is a measure of how fast events pass or how long something takes, and it is always moving forward in one direction (from past to future). It can be compared to an imaginary river that flows through us, taking us from our birth to our death. We are all born into this river at some point, then we experience life's events and grow older, and eventually, we reach the end of the river.

Time is measured using various units such as seconds, minutes, hours, days, months, years, and so on. All these units make up what is called "time." But here is the funny thing about time - you cannot see it, touch it, taste it, smell it, or hear it. You only know it exists because of its effects on the rest of the world around you.

The past is where everything happened before you were born. The present is where everything happens right now. The future is where everything will happen after you are gone. Everything else is just

results.prompt: Explain the concept of time to someone who has never encountered the idea.
results.label: writing_09
results.text:
Sure! Let me explain what time is in a simple way.

Imagine you have a big clock on your wall, and it's ticking away. You might notice that the hands move around in circles, but they don't seem to go anywhere. The hands just stay in one place and point to different numbers as if they're making a journey around the face of the clock.

Now, let's think about what happens when the hands move from 12 o'clock to 3 o'clock. It feels like something happened - the hour hand went from pointing at 12 to pointing at 3. But it's not really happening. It's just the hands moving around the clock.

That's kind of what time is. Time isn't really something that passes or moves by itself. It's just a measure of how long things take to happen, or how fast events unfold. We use clocks and calendars to mark off moments and periods of time, just as we use rulers to mark off inches

results.prompt: Describe what makes a city feel alive, drawing on sensory and social details.

results.text:
A city feels alive with a vibrant energy that pulses through its very streets. You can see it in the faces of people hurrying by you - a mix of smiles and frowns, some heads down texting and others looking up to talk. The air smells of coffee, hot dogs, wet pavement and exhaust from cars . The sounds are constant : the honking of horns , the chatter of people , the squeal of brakes and wheels , and music playing from passing taxis and restaurants . It's all moving in one direction - forward .

The buildings themselves tell their own story . You see modern skyscrapers next to old brick ones . They're painted in bright colors or faded . There are high-rise apartments and single-story houses with front porches . Some have signs on windows advertising deals , others are dark and empty .

results.prompt: Write a letter from a tree to the person who planted it fifty years ago.

results.text:
From your roots deep, I am still here. My trunk is now a solid grey, and my leaves have grown so large they nearly cover the ground. I am old and wise now, with stories to tell of the life you gave me. 

I do not know your name, but I feel like I can see your face in the branches, as if I had never been parted. Do you remember when we were young? When we just met? When you were so young, and everything was new and bright. 

I watched you plant me with such love and care, and I felt so happy. But then, as you left to go back to your life, I felt alone. I missed you, and I cried. 

But I did not give up. I grew, and I grew. And now, fifty years later, I am still here. I am proud of all that I have become, and I am glad that you are no longer here to see me.

results.prompt: Write a letter from a tree to the person who planted it fifty years ago.
results.label: writing_13
results.text:
A few days before the last frost of winter, you were walking in the park when you saw a young tree, growing by the side of a pond. It had leaves the colour of spring, green and bright, with a hint of yellow, like the ones on my own head. You didn't think much of it at first, but I can tell that you were struck by the beauty of the thing, for I was your last stop.

The leaves fell off when the cold weather came, but I didn't think you'd ever come back. But one day, as the sun shone on my head, you looked over at me with such a look in your eyes, that I knew I would never be forgotten. And so, here I am, fifty years later, still standing tall, proud and strong.

You gave me life, when you gave me home, and here I am, still alive. I've seen you grow into a young man, into a man, into a father

results.prompt: Describe a moment of unexpected kindness you witnessed or imagined.
results.label: writing_16
results.text:
I vividly remember an incident that occurred one rainy afternoon in the bustling city. It was a cold and dreary day, and most people were hurrying through their daily routines, seeking shelter from the pouring rain. Amidst this rush, there was a man standing outside a bookstore, his umbrella collapsed, and his shoes were wet. He looked disheveled and was holding a cardboard sign with a simple message: "I need help."

As I approached him, he appeared to be deeply troubled and discouraged. But then, something remarkable happened. A young woman, who was walking by, stopped and asked if she could assist him. She offered to give him an umbrella from her bag, which was still partly open, and helped him put on an extra sweater that she had brought along for herself.

I'll take you to the moment of an unexpected kindness, which happened during my college days.

It was a chilly winter afternoon, and I had just walked out of class to get lunch. The campus cafe was empty as usual. I had no money on me. I wanted to make it home for dinner with my family. I looked at my phone but saw no signal - I had lost it somewhere in the building.

The moment was one that was unexpectedly filled with a deep sense of compassion and love, yet it didn't come from someone we would normally associate with such feelings. It was the moment I saw a man in his mid-30s sitting alone on a bench at the park, reading a book under the shade of a tree. He had a slight frown on his face and looked lost in thought.

I walked by him and was about to leave when I noticed a small dog barking at him from a distance. I could tell he wasn't happy about it. I decided to stop and see if there was anything I could do.

As I approached him, the dog came running towards me. It was clear that the dog was very upset, but it also seemed to be following my every move. I bent down and put my hand out for the dog to sniff. It hesitated for a moment before sniffing my hand and wagging its tail.

results.prompt: Describe a moment of unexpected kindness you witnessed or imagined.
results.label: writing_16
results.text:
The kind of thing that would make your day a little better.

A couple weeks ago I was on the 23/4 train and it was full of people. I had a pretty tight schedule to get home, but I sat at the back anyway because I was a bit tired.

I'm not usually a nice person to strangers, so when I saw this guy, he was standing next to me, I thought he was going to sit in front of me. But he just stood there smiling at me. Then he said "I've been watching you for a while, I noticed you seemed really stressed out, do you need a hug?"

And then he gave me a big hug and told me to stop stressing out and to enjoy the journey.

I don't know what happened before him but I guess he was just sitting behind me, I didn't even know he was there. It made my whole ride so much better.

Subject: Declining the Job Offer

Dear [Hiring Manager’s Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my gratitude for the opportunity to interview and consider a position with [Company Name]. Your team and the role you described were very appealing, and I was excited about the potential to contribute to such an innovative organization.

After careful consideration of all aspects, including the details of the offer, my current situation, and some recent personal developments, I have decided that it would be best for me to decline the position at this time. results.prompt: Write a professional email declining a job offer while maintaining a positive relationship.
results.label: writing_17
results.text:
Subject: No thanks for the position 

Hi [Hr name]

I have been thinking about your job offer for quite some time , and I really appreciate the opportunity . After much deliberation , I have decided to not take up this offer .

The main reason is that my current job is going to be ending in next month and i would like to keep that going till the end of this year and start a new job at the beginning of january 2019.

I have also looked into other positions and found another opportunity in a different field which has a bit better match for me and I believe I will do well in that role . 

So please accept my apologies for not accepting this offer but I hope you understand and that we can stay as friends / colleagues etc.  

Thank You

Best regards 
[ Your Name ]

That is great to hear. The company and the person are great . So many things lined up for me to leave my current job at the end of this month
